subject: [PATCH v2 1/7] mm: list_lru: lock_list_lru_of_memcg() cannot return NULL if !skip_empty

reviews.author: Shakeel Butt
reviews.summary: Shakeel Butt noted that the lock_list_lru_of_memcg() function cannot return NULL if !skip_empty as per the original patch description, but the updated code still contains a path where it can return NULL when skip_empty is false. He requested to remove the NULL check in this case.

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged a concern about the complexity of adding another level to the lock function stack and is unsure if it's worth the effort.

reviews.author: Shakeel Butt
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Shakeel Butt suggested reducing the number of parameters in lock_list_lru_of_memcg() based on a discussion with David, but did not provide specific technical details or suggestions for improvement.

Reviewer Shakeel Butt expressed agreement with Johannes Weiner's patch and stated he would review the remaining patches in the next version, indicating no specific technical concerns or objections.
reviews.raw_body:
Yeah, I saw your discussion with David and thought on how can we further reduce
the params.

---

I am fine with whatever route you take. I know you have next version ready to
send, I will review the remaining patches for the next version (though I have
taken a look on the current series but will add my tags for the next one :P).

2026-03-12.patch_summary: This patch removes unnecessary NULL checks in the list_lru_add and list_lru_del functions, as acquiring a lock on the list LRU will never fail when walking up the cgroup hierarchy or falling back to the node list.
